SEARCH
Package com.nib.commons.storage

Class AbstractNNumeric

java.lang.Object
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNField
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNElementary
com.nib.commons.storage.AbstractNNumeric
All Implemented Interfaces:
IDataArea, Cloneable
Direct Known Subclasses:
AbstractNDecimal, AbstractNFloatingPoint, NInteger
public abstract class AbstractNNumeric extends AbstractNElementary
Represents an abstract representation of a numeric field. This class provides methods to read, write, compare, and manipulate numeric values with various types such as BigDecimal, int, long, double, and other field types. It also provides comparison and equality checks between numeric values. This class is expected to be extended and implemented with specific logic for handling numeric fields.

  • Nested Class Summary

    • zeros

      public AbstractNField zeros()
      Sets the current value to zero and returns the instance of the field.
      Overrides:
      zeros in class AbstractNField
      Returns:
      the current instance of AbstractNField with its value set to zero.

    • setValue

      public void setValue(AbstractNNumeric value)
      Sets the value of the numeric field using another AbstractNNumeric.
      Parameters:
      value - the AbstractNNumeric to set

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NChar value)
      Sets the value of the current field using the provided NChar instance. Adjusts offsets if the length of the provided value is different from the length of the current field. If the provided value is shorter, the current field is zeroed out before the value is applied.
      Parameters:
      value - the NChar instance to be set as the current object's value

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NGroup value)
      Sets the value of this field using the provided NGroup value. Adjusts offsets for alignment based on the length of the provided value compared to the current field's length. If the provided value is longer, it offsets the source. If it is shorter, it fills the current field with zeros and offsets the target.
      Parameters:
      value - the NGroup field from which the value is to be set

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NBinary value)
      Sets the value of the numeric field using an NBinary object.
      Parameters:
      value - the NBinary object to set

    • setValue

      public void setValue(NIndex value)
      Sets the value of the numeric field using a NIndex.
      Parameters:
      value - the NIndex to set

      Parameters:
      by - the double value to divide by
      giving - the AbstractNNumeric to store the result

    • divideBy

      public void divideBy(BigDecimal by, AbstractNNumeric giving, AbstractNNumeric remainder)
      Divides this numeric field by a BigDecimal value and stores the result and remainder in the given AbstractNNumerics.
      Parameters:
      by - the BigDecimal value to divide by
      giving - the AbstractNNumeric to store the result
      remainder - the AbstractNNumeric to store the remainder

    • divideBy

      public void divideBy(long by, AbstractNNumeric giving, AbstractNNumeric remainder)
      Divides this numeric field by a long value and stores the result and remainder in the given AbstractNNumerics.
      Parameters:
      by - the long value to divide by
      giving - the AbstractNNumeric to store the result
      remainder - the AbstractNNumeric to store the remainder

    • inRange

      public boolean inRange(int downLimit, int upLimit)
      Checks if the numeric field is within the specified range.
      Parameters:
      downLimit - the lower bound of the range
      upLimit - the upper bound of the range
      Returns:
      true if the numeric field is within the range, false otherwise

    • setValue

      public void setValue(char[] value)
      Sets the value of the numeric field using a character array.
      Parameters:
      value - the character array to set

    • getDateOfInteger

      public StringValue getDateOfInteger()
      Gets the date representation of the integer value of the numeric field.
      Returns:
      the date representation as a StringValue

    • getIntegerOfDate

      public int getIntegerOfDate()
      Gets the integer representation of the date value of the numeric field.
      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • getDateToYYYYMMDD

      public int getDateToYYYYMMDD()
      Returns an integer value representing a date. The DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD function converts the field value from a date with a two-digit year (YYnnnn) to a date with a four-digit year (YYYYnnnn).

      The function type is integer.

      Format Read syntax diagramSkip visual syntax diagram FUNCTION DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD

      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • getDateToYYYYMMDD

      public int getDateToYYYYMMDD(int window)
      Returns an integer value representing a date. The DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD function converts the field value from a date with a two-digit year (YYnnnn) to a date with a four-digit year (YYYYnnnn). "window" is added to the year at the time of execution, defines the ending year of a 100-year interval, or sliding century window, into which the year of argument-1 falls.

      The function type is integer.

      Format Read syntax diagramSkip visual syntax diagram FUNCTION DATE-TO-YYYYMMDD (window)

      Parameters:
      window - must be an integer. If "window", the function is evaluated assuming the value 50 was specified. The sum of the year at the time of execution and the value of argument-2 must be less than 10,000 and greater than 1,699.
      Returns:
      the integer representation of the date

    • getDateToYYYYMMDD

      public int getDateToYYYYMMDD(AbstractNNumeric window)
